title: Puerto Banus Catamaran Charter Group: Costs and 2026 Tips
html:
Planning a catamaran charter group in Puerto Banus can feel daunting, but with advance booking and a clear budget of EUR 2,500 to EUR 5,000 for a full-day luxury outing, it becomes straightforward. BoatHire24 offers verified listings so you can compare options and secure your ideal vessel from the well-known marina at the heart of Marbella's glamorous coast.

How much does it cost to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus for a group?

A group catamaran charter in Puerto Banus typically costs between EUR 2,500 and EUR 5,000 for a full day, depending on size and season. Shared tours start around EUR 100 per person, while a private 50-foot catamaran for up to 20 guests might cost EUR 2,000 for a half-day in June. In high season (July to September) rates can rise by 20-30 per cent. For a premium experience, booking through yacht charter Puerto Banus listings on BoatHire24 ensures you get the best deals and transparent pricing.

What is included in the cost?

Most charters include a skipper, fuel, and basic insurance. Premium packages add a host, catering, and water toys like paddleboards. Always check the listing for inclusions before confirming.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

The best departure points are Puerto Banus marina, Marbella Port, and the nearby coastal towns of Estepona and Benalmadena. From Puerto Banus you can head east to Cabopino Beach or west to the exclusive Sotogrande area. The Bay of Algeciras is a 30-nautical-mile journey south, offering a full-day excursion. Most group charters begin at the Puerto Banus dinghy dock, where the crew greet you directly.

Which boat to rent?

  1. Lagoon 450 catamaran, ideal for a group of 8-10, from EUR 2,500 per full day with two four-berth cabins and a flybridge.
  2. Azimut 50 flybridge, a motor yacht for 12 guests on day trips, from EUR 3,200 per full day including crew.
  3. Sunseeker Predator 74, a high-performance motor yacht for 10 guests, from EUR 4,500 per full day with tender and jet ski.
  4. Fairline Squadron 58, a luxury motor yacht for 8 guests, from EUR 3,800 per full day with full air conditioning.
  5. Astondoa 112, a superyacht experience for 20 guests, from EUR 8,000 per full day, often used for corporate events.

Costs of a catamaran charter group in Puerto Banus

  • Shared guided tour from EUR 100 per person for a 3-hour cruise;
  • Small motorboat (up to 6 guests) for a half-day from EUR 600;
  • Catamaran charter for a full day (up to 20 guests) from EUR 2,500;
  • CreAAed luxury yacht (up to 30 guests) from EUR 5,000 per full day.

For a direct comparison of large catamarans, you can view the Shangani listing on BoatHire24, which includes detailed pricing and availability for groups.

Season and licence requirements

The high season runs from late June to early September, with the Levante wind often strengthening in the afternoon. From October to May, rates drop by roughly 30 per cent. Regarding licences, guests on a skippered charter need no licence, the operator holds the required credentials. For bareboat hire in Spanish waters, you generally need an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) and a VHF radio licence, but the operator confirms exact requirements.

“Book a morning departure to avoid the afternoon Levante wind and enjoy calmer seas around Cabopino, the skipper can moor for a swim stop at the Mar Menor beach.”

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 100, 8,000 per day per boat
  • Peak months: July to September 2026
  • Trip length: 3 hours to full day (8-10 hours)
  • Guest capacity: 6, 30 on selected vessels
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I charter a catamaran in Puerto Banus for a large group of 20?

Yes, many motor yachts and catamarans, such as the Astondoa 112 or the Shangani, accommodate up to 20 guests for day charters.

What is the best month for a catamaran charter in Puerto Banus?

May, June, and September offer warm weather, lower crowds, and more stable sea conditions than July or August.

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Puerto Banus?

If you charter a skippered boat, no licence is needed; for bareboat hire you typically require an ICC and a VHF licence, but the operator confirms requirements for each booking.

How far can we sail in a half-day from Puerto Banus?

In a half day (4 hours) you can reach Cabopino Beach or Marbella’s eastern coves, roughly 12 to 15 nautical miles round trip.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Puerto Banus or list your own. To find the perfect vessel for your group, browse current catamaran offers on BoatHire24, and if you own a boat, consider listing it to reach thousands of travellers planning their 2026 holidays.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Puerto Banus and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
